forecasts.question: Will AGI be developed before 1 January 2030?
forecasts.key_change: → Stable; Metaculus median steady at mid-2028; no new signals today
forecasts.base_rate: Metaculus community: 25% by 2029. Manifold: 47% before 2028. Combined forecast from multiple sources: AGI by 2031 (50%). Historical base rate for transformative tech predictions: ~30% arrive on time.

factors_up.detail: Metaculus median at March 2028; accelerating expert consensus
Manifold at 47% before 2028; prediction markets pricing higher
Dario Amodei and Demis Hassabis both predict 3-5 years

factors_down.detail: Yann LeCun says 'decades away'; fundamental architecture debates unresolved
No consensus on what counts as AGI; definitional ambiguity favours NO
Historical tech predictions systematically early; 3.75 years is very short

key_uncertainties: Whether current scaling laws continue or hit diminishing returns in 2026-2027
Definition of AGI — what counts as a 'credible announcement' vs marketing

revise_up: Major AI lab announces breakthrough on reasoning/planning benchmarks
Multiple labs achieve expert-level performance on novel scientific tasks
revise_down: Evidence of scaling plateau in frontier models
Major labs publicly downgrade AGI timelines

forecasts.question: Will economic consultancy lose ≥33% value/jobs by end 2030 due to AI?
forecasts.key_change: ↑ McKinsey plans 10% workforce cut over 18-24 months; 65% of enterprises say traditional consulting losing value
forecasts.base_rate: No industry has declined 33% in 4 years from technology disruption in modern history. Travel agencies declined ~50% over 15 years. Print media advertising declined ~60% over 10 years. Base rate for this speed of disruption: <5%.

factors_up.detail: McKinsey cutting 10% non-client-facing staff; using thousands of internal AI agents
65% of enterprises say traditional consulting no longer delivers value
Deloitte restructuring job titles; Big Four adapting to AI-driven model

factors_down.detail: 33% is an extremely high threshold; industry is adapting not collapsing
Consulting revenue may grow even as headcount shifts; firms pivoting to AI consulting
Regulatory, trust, and relationship barriers protect high-end advisory

key_uncertainties: Whether AI replaces consulting output or creates new demand for AI-augmented advisory
Whether revenue or employment is the binding metric (very different trajectories)

revise_up: Major consulting firm reports >15% annual revenue decline
Multiple Big Four announce >20% headcount reductions
revise_down: Consulting industry revenue grows despite AI adoption
AI consulting becomes a major new revenue stream for incumbents

forecasts.question: Will Labour be largest party at next UK General Election?
forecasts.key_change: ↓ Reform lead steady at 7-8pts; multi-party fragmentation deepens with Greens at 17%
forecasts.base_rate: Incumbent UK governing parties won 7 of last 10 general elections. However, mid-term polling deficits of this magnitude (8-10pts) have only been recovered once (1992). Labour-specific: party has never won from this low a polling position with 3+ years to go.

factors_up.detail: FPTP could still deliver Labour most seats even on 25-28% vote share if opposition splits
3+ years until election; incumbents can recover (1992 precedent)
Reform has no incumbency infrastructure; converting polls to seats is harder

factors_down.detail: Labour polling at historic lows: 18-21%; Reform leads at 27-28%
Combined Con+Lab vote share at 38.2%; five-party system makes FPTP unpredictable against Labour
Greens surging to 17%; splitting progressive vote further

key_uncertainties: Whether Reform UK can maintain polling lead through to election and convert to seats under FPTP
Whether Labour can consolidate the progressive vote as election approaches

revise_up: Labour recovers to within 3pts of Reform in sustained polling
Reform suffers major scandal or internal split
revise_down: Labour falls below 15% in sustained polling
Reform wins major by-election in former Labour seat

forecasts.question: Will AGI be developed before 1 January 2035?
forecasts.key_change: → Stable; Metaculus 50% by 2033 consensus holds; 8.75 years is long horizon
forecasts.base_rate: Metaculus: 50% by 2033, implying well above 50% by 2035. Combined multi-source forecast: 80% by 2035. Expert survey median: 2030-2035 range. Longer timeline significantly increases probability.

factors_up.detail: Metaculus 50% by 2033; 2 extra years adds significant probability mass
Current pace of AI progress exceeding most 2023-era predictions
Massive capital investment ($100B+ annually) sustaining scaling

factors_down.detail: Fundamental architecture debates remain; current approaches may plateau
Definitional ambiguity; 'credible announcement' is a high bar
Geopolitical or regulatory disruption could slow development

key_uncertainties: Whether current transformer/scaling paradigm can reach AGI or requires novel architecture
How geopolitical competition and AI regulation evolve over next 8 years

revise_up: AI systems achieve novel scientific discoveries autonomously
revise_down: Evidence of sustained scaling plateau across multiple labs
Major shift to 'AGI requires fundamentally new approach' consensus

forecasts.question: Will China initiate a military blockade or invasion of Taiwan before 1 January 2030?
forecasts.key_change: ↓ PLAAF incursions at lowest levels since early 2020s; US intel reaffirms attack unlikely
forecasts.base_rate: Historical base rate for major power initiating military action against defended territory: ~2-3% per year. Over 3.75 years: ~8-11%. Prediction markets (Metaculus/Polymarket): 10-15% range.

factors_up.detail: China conducted explicit blockade rehearsal drills (Justice Mission 2025) with 14 warships, 130 aircraft
PRC military modernisation on track for 2027 capability milestone
US-China strategic competition intensifying; Taiwan chip leverage

factors_down.detail: US intelligence assessment: imminent attack unlikely; China prefers non-force unification
PLAAF activity near Taiwan at lowest since early 2020s; 17 days of zero activity Feb-Mar
Amphibious assault recognised as extremely difficult; high failure risk if US intervenes

key_uncertainties: Whether PRC leadership calculates that a window of opportunity is closing as Taiwan strengthens defenses
How US political dynamics and alliance commitments evolve through 2029

revise_up: China deploys amphibious forces to positions consistent with invasion staging
Major diplomatic breakdown between Beijing and Taipei
revise_down: China reduces military exercises around Taiwan for sustained period

forecasts.question: Will UK annual CPI inflation fall below 2.0% for at least one month before 1 January 2027?
forecasts.key_change: ↑ BoE Feb report forecasts CPI falling to 2.1% in Q2; Ofgem cap cut to £1,616 in April creates downside risk
forecasts.base_rate: UK CPI has been below 2.0% for extended periods (2015-2016, 2019-2020). When BoE forecasts 2.1%, actual readings have come in below forecast ~35% of the time. January 2026 was 3.0%, so significant fall needed.

factors_up.detail: BoE forecasts 2.1% in Q2 2026; only 0.1pp from threshold
Ofgem cap falling to £1,616 in April (from £1,758); energy disinflation significant
9 months of data remaining (Apr-Dec 2026); multiple chances to dip below 2.0%

factors_down.detail: January 2026 CPI at 3.0%; February expected ~3%; still far from 2.0%
OBR central forecast is 2.1% not below 2.0%; BoE also at 2.1%
Services inflation remains sticky; wage growth still elevated

key_uncertainties: Whether the April energy price cap cut overshoots and drives CPI below 2.0% in Q2 data
Whether services inflation (the sticky component) eases faster than forecast

revise_up: February CPI comes in below 2.8% (tomorrow's release)
Further energy price falls beyond current Ofgem cap
revise_down: Oil price spike or trade tariffs push costs up

forecasts.question: Will Spider-Man: Brand New Day gross over $1.5B worldwide by 31 December 2026?
forecasts.key_change: → Stable; 4+ months to release; record 718M trailer views; no new tracking data
forecasts.base_rate: Of the 12 MCU films grossing >$1B, 6 crossed $1.5B (50%). Spider-Man: No Way Home hit $1.92B. Tom Holland Spider-Man trilogy averaged $1.28B. Films with record trailer views: 4/5 crossed $1.5B.

factors_up.detail: Record-breaking trailer: 718.6M views in 24hrs (nearly 2x previous record)
No Way Home grossed $1.92B; strong franchise momentum
Tom Holland return after 5-year gap creates event-film status

factors_down.detail: MCU fatigue: several recent MCU films underperformed expectations
July release faces summer competition; not holiday season
One analyst predicts Supergirl could outgross it; competitive landscape uncertain

key_uncertainties: Whether trailer hype translates to sustained theatrical demand vs front-loaded opening
How much MCU fatigue affects Spider-Man specifically (historically more resistant than other MCU properties)

revise_up: Advance ticket sales break records when they open
Second trailer maintains or increases hype levels
revise_down: Major competing blockbuster moves to same release window

forecasts.question: Will OpenAI and Anthropic have a combined ARR exceeding $70B by 31 December 2026?
forecasts.key_change: ↑ Anthropic hits $19B ARR (Mar); OpenAI at $25B (Feb); combined $44B with 9 months to go
forecasts.base_rate: No SaaS company has grown from $44B to $70B ARR in 9 months. However, AI companies are in unprecedented growth phase. Anthropic grew from $9B to $19B in 3 months (111% in one quarter).

factors_up.detail: Anthropic at $19B ARR and accelerating; grew $10B in 3 months
OpenAI at $25B ARR; on track for $35-40B by year-end at 3.4x annual growth
Claude Code alone at $2.5B run-rate; enterprise adoption accelerating (7x YoY in $100K+ customers)

factors_down.detail: Growth rates typically decelerate; doubling from $44B to $70B in 9 months is ambitious
Anthropic's own target is $26B for year-end (not $35B+)
Competition from Google, Meta open-source could cap pricing power

key_uncertainties: Whether Anthropic's extraordinary growth rate sustains or decelerates sharply
Whether OpenAI can maintain 3.4x annual growth as base gets larger

revise_up: Anthropic or OpenAI reports ARR exceeding $30B before July
New enterprise product launches drive step-change in revenue
revise_down: Either company reports growth deceleration below 2x annual
Major customer churn or pricing pressure from open-source competitors

forecasts.question: Will UK average annual real GDP growth exceed 1.5% across the 2030s (2030-2039)?
forecasts.key_change: → Stable; OBR medium-term forecasts remain at 1.4-1.6% range; right at threshold
forecasts.base_rate: UK average real GDP growth: 2010s: 1.7%, 2000s: 1.8%, 1990s: 2.5%. OBR March 2026 forecasts 1.5-1.6% for late 2020s. IMF projects 1.5% for 2027. Post-financial-crisis trend growth ~1.5%.

factors_up.detail: AI productivity gains could boost trend growth by 0.3-0.5pp if widely adopted
OBR forecasts 1.6% for 2027-2028; above threshold in near-term
UK services sector well-positioned for AI-driven growth

factors_down.detail: OBR medium-term average ~1.4%; below threshold
Aging demographics reducing labour force growth
Post-Brexit trade friction constraining potential growth

key_uncertainties: Whether AI-driven productivity gains materialise at scale in the 2030s
Whether UK fiscal and trade policy environment improves or deteriorates

revise_up: OBR or BoE raises long-run growth forecast above 1.6%
Evidence of AI-driven productivity acceleration in UK economy
revise_down: UK enters recession or sustained period of sub-1% growth
